relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: A Levite household becomes the hidden doorway of liberation.
A mother sees goodness and hides life for three months.
A tiny ark is sealed and placed among the reeds.
The sister watches from distance, keeping witness.
Pharaoh's daughter descends to the river and sees the basket.
Compassion opens where empire commanded death.
The sister offers a path for the child to be nursed by his own mother.
The mother is quietly restored to the child.
The empire's house unknowingly pays for the child's life to be nourished.
Moses is named as one drawn from the waters.
He grows and sees the burdens of his siblings.
His first intervention comes from outrage but is hidden in sand.
He sees conflict among his own people and asks why one strikes the other.
The hidden violence is exposed, and Moses fears.
He flees Pharaoh and becomes a sojourner by a well.
Seven daughters come to draw water.
Moses rises to help them and water the flock.
Their father notices the sudden ease.
They name Moses as rescuer and water-drawer.
Hospitality calls the stranger to bread.
Moses dwells in Midian and receives Zipporah.
Gershom names his exile: sojourner in foreign land.
Israel's groaning rises from crushing service.
The Divine hears and remembers covenant.
The Divine sees, and the Divine knows.

contemplative_note: Exodus 2 is rescue through hidden hands: mother, sister, princess, servant, daughters, wells, exile, groaning, and the Divine who hears, remembers, sees, and knows.
